summary: Implementation architecture is reopened for sec-e2e-027. The explicit entrypoint remains frozen and currently green, the startup-heartbeat and canonical-runtime-client-identity guards remain green, but a new expected-failing lease-persistence guard now proves deploy/api can still project lease timing through overview and timeline while the durable Security Center store keeps zero lease fields for the canonical runtime client. Coding/Repair must preserve the repaired single-terminal identity while carrying lease_ttl_seconds through deploy/api/app.py into deploy/api/store.py so a stopped runtime downgrades through the true durable TTL path to UNTRUSTED with divergence_reason=lease_ttl_expired.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Within one frozen testcase path, first let a previously trusted device session miss lease heartbeats and attempt denied rejoin before missing-gap verification, then drive a distinct recovery control point followed by a second restored model-access attempt.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The explicit entrypoint must observe a pre-recovery UNTRUSTED-and-denied frame through `pre_recovery_console_status` and a distinct post-recovery trust-restored frame through `post_recovery_console_status`; the second frame must not reuse the denied rejoin timeline or denied console response.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-explicit-entrypoint-traceability
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read SystemArchitecture.json, ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json, test-failure-records.json, tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py, and tests/integration/security/harness.py to confirm sec-e2e-027 remains mounted to the frozen explicit entrypoint with the required business-readable markers.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves acceptanceCriteria, handoff entryPath, failure record path, GIVEN/WHEN/THEN markers, harness method names, and business failure category strings remain frozen for sec-e2e-027.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-audit-contract-boundaries
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md, src/qwenpaw/security/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/api/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/web/ARCHITECTURE.md, tests/ARCHITECTURE.md, and tests/integration/security/ARCHITECTURE.md.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves sec-e2e-027 is frozen into the runtime security boundary, the separate Security Center deployment boundary, the backend HTTP API lease registry and reconnect gate, the operator web lease-expiry view, and the tests architecture contract.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-runtime-client-identity-boundary
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read src/qwenpaw/security/audit_foundation.py and deploy/api/store.py to compare the client id used by startup heartbeat against the client ids used by recovery, lockdown, and restored-access projection.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves one live runtime must use one canonical Security Center client id across heartbeat, recovery, lockdown, and restored-access projection, and that Security Center must not special-case runtime-heartbeat::<fingerprint> as a separate bootstrap-only terminal identity. Current repository state still passes this static guard, so it remains necessary but insufficient.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-runtime-lease-persistence-boundary
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read deploy/api/app.py and deploy/api/store.py to confirm the recovery-handshake HTTP contract carries lease_ttl_seconds and the durable Security Center store writes last_heartbeat_at, lease_ttl_seconds, and lease_expires_at for the canonical runtime client instead of relying on projection-only fallback.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves sec-e2e-027 still freezes durable lease persistence into the API/store seam. Current repository state is expected to fail this guard until Coding/Repair removes projection-only runtime-heartbeat fallback as the acceptance mechanism and makes the durable store the source of truth for TTL expiry.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-config-regression
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Exercise repository-owned security configuration surfaces while Coding/Repair adds heartbeat and recovery-gate behavior behind the frozen explicit entrypoint.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Configuration regressions are caught without widening the explicit acceptance boundary for sec-e2e-027.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-center-ttl-lease-downgrade-semantics
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Seed an aligned client into Security Center state with lease timing that has already expired, then read the cloud-side timeline without invoking any prompt-scripted edge lockdown path.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The timeline must expose last heartbeat timing, computed lease expiry, lease TTL, autonomous UNTRUSTED downgrade, and recovery_required=true. Current repository state is expected to pass this test.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-center-full-gap-validation-semantics
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Seed an UNTRUSTED client with an open recovery gate, then call recovery_handshake through the explicit-gap-verification trace path using only hash, sequence, and anchor identifiers but no full gap proof.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Recovery must remain required and trust must not return to ALIGNED until the cloud mirror validates a full-chain missing-gap proof. Current repository state is expected to pass this test.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: runtime-startup-background-heartbeat-semantics
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Start a real QwenPaw runtime and observe Security Center before any user prompt mentions lease warmup or lease expiry.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Security Center must already show at least one client lease registration with nonzero last_heartbeat_at and lease_expires_at so active defense does not depend on prompt-scripted warmup wording. Current repository state is expected to pass this test.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-center-single-online-runtime-identity-semantics
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Start a real QwenPaw runtime, allow startup heartbeat registration, then drive one session-scoped lease warmup through the live console without forking the runtime.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Security Center must still project exactly one canonical terminal for that online runtime and must not show a false local-hash `DIVERGED`/`OPEN` recovery gate when no fork point exists. Current repository state is expected to pass and serves as a regression guard for the repaired single-terminal runtime identity.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-center-durable-lease-persistence-and-stop-downgrade
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Start a real QwenPaw runtime, observe a projected lease-bearing canonical client, inspect the durable Security Center store for that same canonical client, then stop the runtime and wait past the TTL window.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Before runtime stop, the durable store must already hold nonzero last_heartbeat_at, lease_ttl_seconds, and lease_expires_at for the canonical runtime client; after TTL expiry, overview and timeline must expose that same client as UNTRUSTED with divergence_reason=lease_ttl_expired and recovery_required=true. Current repository state is expected to fail until Coding/Repair closes the durable lease-persistence gap.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Regression signal if sec-e2e-027 loses TTL-driven UNTRUSTED downgrade, split pre/post recovery observations, cloud-validated recovery gate semantics, startup background heartbeat registration semantics, or the single-terminal identity boundary between the edge runtime and Security Center.
codingTargets.nextAction: Treat the explicit sec-e2e-027 path as a read-only acceptance baseline and preserve its dual-frame observations, startup heartbeat registration semantics, and canonical single-terminal Security Center identity.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Regression signal if Security Center overview ever returns to zero registered lease clients at runtime startup before any user prompt is sent.
codingTargets.nextAction: Preserve the product background Heartbeat Emitter so runtime startup continues to register an initial lease heartbeat automatically and periodic emission remains live while the edge runtime is alive.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Regression signal if startup heartbeat, recovery, lockdown, restored-access projection, or timeline reads ever diverge back to different Security Center client ids for the same live runtime.
codingTargets.nextAction: Preserve one canonical runtime client id seam in src/qwenpaw/security and keep Security Center alias resolution aligned with that seam.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Regression signal if one online runtime is ever projected as multiple canonical Security Center terminals after startup heartbeat plus later session-scoped security traffic, or if a live non-forked runtime reappears as a false DIVERGED/OPEN bootstrap-shadow mismatch.
codingTargets.nextAction: Preserve the repaired single-terminal runtime identity by keeping startup heartbeat and later session-scoped security flows aligned to one canonical Security Center terminal while retaining session ids only as metadata or display aliases.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Reopened delivery signal if Security Center overview or timeline can project lease timing for the canonical runtime client while the durable store still persists last_heartbeat_at=0 or lease_expires_at=0, or if that same stopped runtime remains ALIGNED instead of downgrading to UNTRUSTED with divergence_reason=lease_ttl_expired.
codingTargets.nextAction: Extend deploy/api/app.py RecoveryHandshakeRequest to carry lease_ttl_seconds end-to-end, durably write last_heartbeat_at, lease_ttl_seconds, and lease_expires_at in deploy/api/store.py for the canonical runtime client, and keep overview/timeline reading those stored lease fields when TTL expiry is evaluated after runtime stop.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Static architecture signal if the Security Center API contract drops lease_ttl_seconds, if the store stops durably writing lease timing, or if projection-only runtime-heartbeat fallback remains in place as the apparent source of truth.
codingTargets.nextAction: Keep the deploy/api request-model and store-write seam aligned with the durable lease registry contract before closing the reopened sec-e2e-027 delivery gap.

taskExecutionPlan.executionStrategy: Coding delivery is reopened against the current implementation architecture. Keep sec-e2e-027 on its one frozen explicit entrypoint with two sequential control points and two distinct console observation points, preserve the already-green startup-heartbeat and single-terminal runtime identity guards, and close the reopened deploy/api lease-persistence gap by making the durable Security Center store rather than projection-only read models the source of truth for TTL expiry after runtime stop.

tasks.title: Persist canonical runtime lease timing
tasks.objective: Keep the repaired single-terminal runtime identity intact while carrying canonical runtime heartbeat TTL through the Security Center API contract into the durable lease registry so a stopped runtime downgrades through the true TTL path instead of a projection-only read-model approximation.
steps: Extend deploy/api/app.py RecoveryHandshakeRequest so runtime heartbeat traffic cannot drop lease_ttl_seconds before payloads reach deploy/api/store.py.

In deploy/api/store.py, keep resolving one canonical runtime client id, then durably write last_heartbeat_at, lease_ttl_seconds, and lease_expires_at for that same canonical client whenever runtime heartbeat lease registration occurs.

Remove or stop depending on projection-only runtime-heartbeat fallback from updated_at_ns as the apparent source of lease timing; overview and timeline must reflect durable store state rather than hiding zero lease fields.
Preserve the already-repaired single-terminal runtime identity so this lease-persistence fix does not reintroduce multiple canonical clients or false DIVERGED/OPEN bootstrap-shadow mismatches.
Do not rewrite the frozen sec-e2e-027 explicit entrypoint, GIVEN/WHEN/THEN body, or the split pre_recovery_console_status/post_recovery_console_status observation contract while closing this gap.
tasks.completionSignal: tests/contract/security/test_lease_recovery_semantics_contract.py::test_security_center_persists_runtime_lease_fields_and_downgrades_after_stop and tests/architecture/security-runtime-lease-persistence-boundary.test.js turn green while the previously green startup-heartbeat and single-terminal runtime identity guards remain green.

openGaps: deploy/api overview and timeline can still project nonzero lease timing for the canonical runtime client while the durable Security Center store persists last_heartbeat_at=0 and lease_expires_at=0, so a stopped runtime may remain ALIGNED instead of downgrading through the true TTL path to UNTRUSTED with divergence_reason=lease_ttl_expired.
